content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


July 19, 1993


Karen Lundgren
2594 Irvington Avenue
San Bernardino, CA 92407

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-93-250

Dear Ms. Lundgren:

I am writing to confirm our telephone conversation on
July 14, 1993 wherein we discussed your written request for
advice. You are involved with a campaign committee which supports
a candidate for the San Bernardino County Superintendent of
Schools. The Office of the County Superintendent of Schools
currently does business with many vendors. You inquired as to
whether there are contribution limits on the amount of money a
vendor may contribute to the candidate's committee.

I informed you that under the Political Reform Act, there are
no longer contribution limits unless it is a special election. I
also suggested that you ascertain if there is a local ordinance
which establishes local contribution limits.

I trust this answers your question.

Sincerely,

Jeff Marschner
General Counsel

By: [illegible]
Jill Stecher
Counsel, Legal Division

JM:JS:ak


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

QUESTION: Whether there are contribution limits on the amount of money a vendor doing business with the Office of the County Superintendent of Schools may contribute to a candidate's campaign committee.

CONCLUSION: Under the Political Reform Act, there are no longer contribution limits unless it is a special election. The requester should also ascertain if there is a local ordinance establishing local contribution limits.

The Office of the County Superintendent of Schools currently does business with many vendors. You inquired as to whether there are contribution limits on the amount of money a vendor may contribute to the candidate's committee. embedding.summary: FPPC General Counsel confirms in this advice letter that there are no state contribution limits under the Political Reform Act for contributions to a San Bernardino County Superintendent of Schools candidate committee, except in special elections, though local ordinances may impose additional limits.
